poster_title: MEMTRACK: Evaluating Long-Term Memory and State Tracking in Multi-Platform Dynamic Agent Environments
authors: Darshan Deshpande, Varun Gangal, Hersh Mehta, Anand Kannappan, Rebecca Qian Peng, Wang Patronus AI
paper_info: 39th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2025) Workshop: Scaling Environments for Agents (SEA). arXiv:2510.01353v1 [cs.AI] 1 Oct 2025

content: Context and memory benchmarking focused on conversational instances.
Evaluating memory in dynamic enterprise environments is crucial.
Existing benchmarks are non-cohesive and susceptible to biases.
Current memory evaluation limited to conversational setups.
Real-world tasks require live context switches and multi-platform reasoning.
Asynchronous tools and environments are essential for realistic testing.
Introduction of MEMTRACK benchmark for multi-platform agent environments.
Integration of asynchronous events across platforms like Slack, Linear, and Git.
Curation of MEMTRACK dataset through expert design and agent-based synthesis.
Experiments on state-of-the-art LLMs and memory backends.
Metrics for Correctness, Efficiency, and Redundancy utilized.
Evaluation of LLMs in complex, multi-platform scenarios.
LLMs struggle with context retention and repeated data reading.
Extensible framework for memory-augmented agent evaluation.
Robust set of asynchronous tools and dockerized environment.
Scalable design for automated data curation.
MEMTRACK highlights error patterns in LLMs regarding memory usage.
Framework suitable for complex organizational settings and multi-agent, multi-platform benchmarking.

Fig1.description: Performance metrics of GPT-5 on the MEMTRACK benchmark
